aliases: Old Chang Kee Seafood Party Platter

english: Old Chang Kee Seafood Party Platter

food_subgroup: Seafood snack platter — breaded and battered seafood items including fish fingers, prawn nuggets, crab nuggets, fishballs, and seafood wraps

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mixed breaded/battered seafood party platter. FOODON_03315091 (seafood food product) used as closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for a mixed seafood snack platter. A0CJW (Crustaceans and products thereof) used as closest parent; platter also contains fish and crab products.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code found for a mixed commercial seafood snack platter product.

serving.description: 1 party platter box (36 pieces total: 6 each of Cheezy Fish Finger, Tiger Prawn, Crab Nugget, Fishball, Prawn Nugget, Seafood Wrap)
serving.natural_language_portion: One party platter box contains 36 pieces across six seafood snack varieties, estimated at approximately 720 g total (approximately 20 g per piece average), suitable for sharing among 4–6 people.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Breaded or battered fish and seafood products (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite); supplemented with HPB Singapore nutrient data for local fried seafood snacks
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Old Chang Kee party platter product. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for breaded fish portions (FDC ID 175168), breaded shrimp (FDC ID 175177), fish balls (HPB Singapore Foodo), and fried seafood snacks. The platter contains six item types in equal proportion (6 pieces each). Values represent a blended per-100g estimate across the full platter. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Breaded and Battered Fried Foods — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fish fingers and breaded seafood products; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Old Chang Kee party platter. GI of 55 is estimated based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for breaded fish fingers (GI ~52–58) and similar fried breaded seafood snacks. The wheat-flour breadcrumb coating is the primary glycemic driver. According to University of Sydney GI Database classification, GI 55 is considered 'low'. GL per serving calculated as (55 × 18.5 × 720 / 100) / 100 = approximately 73.4 g per full platter box serving of 720 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; estimated ~3,456 mg per full platter box)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this platter due0 to its very high sodium load from seasoning, fish paste, and commercial processing; a single shared portion may still contribute substantially to the 2,000 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~55) driven by wheat-flour breadcrumb coating; moderate carbohydrate content (~18.5 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this platter in small portions and account for the carbohydrate contribution from the breaded coating; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and avoiding additional starchy sides can help moderate postprandial glucose response. Despite a low GI, the overall carbohydrate load from a large serving can still impact blood glucose.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, moderate saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g), and deep-fried preparation with trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this deep-fried seafood platter due to its combination of high sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat from commercial frying, all of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk; occasional small shared portions are preferable to regular consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Party Platter Box (Seafood Fiesta) is a large sharing platter sold by Old Chang Kee, a well-known Singaporean snack chain famous for its curry puffs and fried seafood snacks. The box contains 36 pieces in total, comprising six pieces each of six different seafood snack items: Cheezy Fish Finger (a breaded fish fillet strip with cheese flavouring), Tiger Prawn (a whole or halved prawn in a crispy batter), Crab Nugget (a processed crab-meat nugget in breadcrumbs), Fishball (a bouncy fish-paste sphere, either fried or steamed), Prawn Nugget (a minced prawn patty in breadcrumbs), and Seafood Wrap (a pastry or spring-roll-style wrap filled with mixed seafood). All items are deep-fried or commercially heat-processed, giving them a golden, crispy exterior with a moist, savoury seafood interior. The platter is designed for group sharing at parties, gatherings, or celebrations and is priced at SGD 29.80. Nutritionally, the platter is energy-dense (~218 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~11.5 g), moderate fat (~11.2 g), and moderate carbohydrate (~18.5 g) from the breadcrumb and batter coatings. Sodium is high (~480 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low), driven by the wheat-flour coating. The platter is halal-certified and contains major allergens including fish, crustaceans, shellfish, wheat (gluten), and milk (from cheese flavouring). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or shellfish allergies.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Old Chang Kee commercial party platter product. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for breaded fish and shrimp products and HPB Singapore data for local fried seafood snacks. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for breaded fish fingers. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are individually well-characterised in approved sources and the platter composition is clearly described.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates for the full mixed platter (equal proportions of six seafood snack types). Individual item nutrient profiles may differ. 2. Serving size of 720 g is estimated at approximately 20 g per piece × 36 pieces; actual piece weights may vary by item type. 3. Sodium value is high and clinically significant; the full platter box contains an estimated ~3,456 mg sodium, which exceeds the HPB Singapore recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 5. Halal status is based on Old Chang Kee's publicly stated halal certification from MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore). 6. trans_fat_g of 0.2 per 100 g is estimated from USDA data for commercially fried breaded seafood; actual value may differ. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and all micronutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite data and are not directly measured for this product. 8. Cuisine tag in input was 'Curry Puff' reflecting the Old Chang Kee brand category on field survey; the food itself is a seafood snack platter and has been reclassified accordingly.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with composite nutrient estimates from USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore. Added multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Added ontology with closest_parent codes and gap notes. Added health context for all three required conditions.

Iteration 2: Addressed validator feedback for Q4. Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' to align with `glycemic_index` 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database classification (GI <= 55 is low). Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `llm_training.natural_language_description` to reflect the 'low' GI category. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Verified all -1 sentinel values are replaced with estimated numeric values from approved sources. Verified zero-macro rule not triggered. Verified GI is numeric > 0. Verified ontology match_type values are within allowed set. Confirmed PASS on all hard gates and Q1–Q8.
